nodes.context: White supremacist and neo-Nazi organization in the United States, founded and formerly led by Tom Metzger.
White supremacist organization; Tom Metzger was a former Grand Dragon.
Metzger's first group, led in the mid-1970s.

nodes.standard_name: Knights of the Ku Klux Klan
nodes.original_text_name: Knights of the Ku Klux Klan
nodes.context: Klan organization led by David Duke; Metzger joined in 1975.

nodes.standard_name: California Knights of the Ku Klux Klan
nodes.original_text_name: California Knights of the Ku Klux Klan
nodes.context: Metzger's own Klan organization founded in 1980 after leaving the national Klan.
White supremacist group whose members murdered Mulugeta Seraw; allegedly had ties to WAR.
Organization claimed by Richard Campos, a WAR sympathizer, for bombings; Campos was the only member.

edges.evidence_quote: three white Aryan supremacists who were members of East Side White Pride, which allegedly had ties to WAR
edges.context: East Side White Pride allegedly had ties to WAR.

edges.evidence_quote: Calls were made in which it was stated that the bombings were perpetrated by an organization called the Aryan Liberation Front, of which Campos was the only member.
edges.context: A WAR sympathizer claimed bombings were by Aryan Liberation Front, but no direct relationship between the orgs is stated.
